message:
feat(server/mail_ui): parse repeated + comma-separated importance filter params for /search

The mail_ui `/search` route accepts an `imp=` query parameter that
lets callers filter search results by importance level (urgent,
high, etc.). Two shapes were reasonable for a caller to send:

    /search?q=foo&imp=urgent&imp=high       (repeated form)
    /search?q=foo&imp=urgent,high           (comma-separated form)

The old implementation did `extract_query_str_all(query, "imp")`
(handles the repeated form) and then ran each value through
`Importance::parse(s)` inline. It did NOT split on commas — a
caller that sent `imp=urgent,high` got ONE value `"urgent,high"`,
failed `Importance::parse`, and saw an empty importance filter.
The UI also carried the raw strings back to the caller for
"selected facets" display, which meant the second form would
disappear from the rendered facet chips.

Fix: extract the parsing into a new `parse_importance_filter_params(query)`
helper that:

    1. Pulls all `imp=` values via `extract_query_str_all` (repeated form).
    2. Splits each on `,` and trims whitespace (comma-separated form).
    3. Runs each segment through `Importance::parse` and keeps the
       successful matches.

`render_search` now calls the helper, then reconstructs a
`selected_importance: Vec<String>` from the parsed importance values
via `Importance::as_str()` so the facet chips reflect the canonical
form (e.g. `"urgent"`, `"high"`) rather than whatever the caller
typed — no more case drift, no more mixed-form display.

Two new unit tests cover both shapes:

    - `parse_importance_filter_params_accepts_repeated_values`:
      `imp=urgent&imp=high` yields `[Urgent, High]`.
    - `parse_importance_filter_params_accepts_comma_separated_values`:
      `imp=urgent%2Chigh` (URL-encoded comma) yields `[Urgent, High]`.
